sovereign_credit_rating.value: S&P: NR, Moody's: NR, Fitch: CCC-

resource_self_sufficiency_index.methodology: Average of min(production/consumption, 1.0) for energy, food, water, minerals

political_risk_premium_bps.methodology: Sovereign rating implied spread + adjustments for instability, conflict, sanctions, autocracy

market_accessibility_score.methodology: Average of capital_account_openness, stock_market_cap/200, financial_dev_index, ease_of_business/100

name: Ethiopia
executive_summary: Ethiopia is in selective default (SD/Ca/RD) with a 2,700 bps political risk premium. TPLF-government clashes have resumed, threatening the Pretoria peace agreement. Elections scheduled June 1, 2026 in a fraught environment with TPLF legal status revoked. GDP growth at 7.2% appears strong but is distorted by low base effects and data quality concerns.
key_changes: TPLF-government clashes resumed -- Tigray conflict risk (major)
Elections scheduled June 1, 2026 -- TPLF legal status revoked
outlook: Ethiopia faces rising conflict risk ahead of June elections. Debt restructuring remains incomplete. Telecom privatization (Safaricom entry) provides one positive structural story.
investor_implications: Ethiopia is effectively uninvestable at selective default. Avoid all exposure. Monitor debt restructuring progress for eventual recovery opportunity.

active_alerts.title: Ethiopia in selective default with Tigray conflict resumption threatening peace agreement
active_alerts.description: Ethiopia's sovereign credit rating at SD/Ca/RD (selective default) with political risk premium of 2,700 bps. TPLF-government clashes resumed. Elections scheduled for June 1, 2026 in a fraught environment with TPLF legal status revoked. Conditions persist.
